name: Torre des Carregador and torre de Ses Portes — circular through the Ses Salines d’Eivissa y Formentera Natural Park
name_original: Torre des Carregador y torre de Ses Portes — circular por el Parque Natural de Ses Salines d’Eivissa y Formentera
region_intro: Urban hiking trails around Parc natural de ses Salines offer a blend of historical cityscapes and coastal proximity. The region features the ancient fortified city of Ibiza (Dalt Vila), characterized by its cobblestone streets, defensive walls, and elevated positions providing views of the harbor and surrounding areas. These urban paths often connect historical landmarks with waterfront promenades, providing varied walking experiences. The terrain within the city is generally undulating, with some climbs to higher points within Dalt Vila. Best urban hiking trails around Parc natural de ses Salines The most popular urban hiking route is Ibiza Old Town (Dalt Vila) – Portal de ses Taules loop from Hï Ibiza, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the historic Dalt Vila and its ancient gateways. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ibiza Old Town (Dalt Vila) – Arched Walkway in Dalt Vila loop from Ibiza, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This route navigates through the narrow streets and arched walkways of Ibiza's Old Town. Local hikers also love the Ibiza Marina – Ibiza Old Town (Dalt Vila) loop from Ibiza, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail leading through the marina and into the historic center, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Hiking around Parc natural de ses Salines is defined by historic city walls, harbor views, and urban promenades. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate climbs within the city. The routes in Parc natural de ses Salines are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 350 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore Parc natural de ses Salines's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many urban hiking trails are available in Parc natural de ses Salines?
A: There are over 25 urban hiking trails in Parc natural de ses Salines, offering a variety of experiences. Most of these routes are considered easy to moderate, making them accessible for many hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on urban hikes in Parc natural de ses Salines?
A: Urban hikes in this region often feature a mix of paved paths, historic cobblestone streets, and coastal promenades. You'll encounter gentle inclines, especially when exploring areas like Dalt Vila, but generally, the elevation changes are minimal, making for comfortable walks.

Q: Are there any easy urban hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, many urban trails in Parc natural de ses Salines are suitable for beginners and families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibiza Marina – Ibiza Old Town (Dalt Vila) loop from Ibiza&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated easy and offers a pleasant stroll through the city's charming areas. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33847286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dalt Vila — Ibiza Old Town – Portal de ses Taules loop from Ibiza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see on an urban hike in this area?
A: The urban trails around Parc natural de ses Salines are rich in history. You can explore the ancient walls and gates of Dalt Vila, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Look out for historic defense towers like Torre de Sal Rossa and Torre de Ses Portes, which offer panoramic views. The traditional salt production sites and the village of Sa Canal also provide a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there any circular urban walks available?
A: Yes, many of the urban hiking trails in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38241180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibiza Old Town (Dalt Vila) – Portal de ses Taules loop from Hï Ibiza&lt;/a&gt; offers a comprehensive circular route through historic parts of Ibiza Town.

Q: What natural features can be observed during urban hikes near Parc natural de ses Salines?
A: While focusing on urban areas, many trails offer views or access to the natural beauty of the region. You'll often see the stunning coastline, pristine beaches like Platja de Ses Salines, and the unique salt ponds. These salt flats are particularly interesting for birdwatching, especially between August and October when flamingos are present. You might also catch glimpses of the protected Posidonia oceanica meadows from coastal viewpoints.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about urban hiking in Parc natural de ses Salines?
A: The komoot community highly rates urban hikes in Parc natural de ses Salines, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 350 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the blend of historical exploration within Dalt Vila, the beautiful coastal views, and the ease of access to these well-maintained urban paths.

Q: Is public transport available to access the urban hiking trails?
A: Yes, Ibiza Town, which serves as a starting point for many urban hikes, is well-connected by public transport. Local bus services can take you to various points around the city and closer to the Parc natural de ses Salines, making it convenient to access trailheads without a car.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the urban hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. Given the urban nature of these trails, especially those around Ibiza Town and its marina, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and bars. Many establishments offer outdoor seating, perfect for a refreshment after your walk, often with scenic views of the harbor or historic streets.

Q: Can I combine an urban hike with a visit to a natural monument?
A: Yes, several urban routes offer easy access to natural monuments. For instance, some trails provide views of or lead close to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Salt Flats of Ibiza&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/448087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Salines Beach&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to experience both the urban environment and the protected natural landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for urban hiking in Parc natural de ses Salines?
A: The shoulder seasons (spring and autumn) are generally ideal for urban hiking in Parc natural de ses Salines. The weather is pleasantly mild, avoiding the intense heat of summer, and the crowds are typically smaller. This allows for comfortable exploration of both the urban centers and the adjacent natural areas.
tips: On the grounds of the parish of Sant Francesc de s'Estany. From here you have a beautiful view of the island of Formentera in the front, the full length of the beach to the left and the town of Ibiza in the background. This watchtower was built in the sixteenth century.
